methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $19M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
MaverickX is a seed-stage climate and energy company founded by Eric Herrera and Jesse Evans that has raised $19M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (LNZA), including series-A stage peers Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M), alongside later-stage private benchmarks like Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Helion, and Redwood Materials.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 2.5x multiplier to the $19M raised proxy, implying a $48M valuation. Peer-median analysis across the three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a $150M median valuation, with a range of $110M–$200M. The peer-median method reflects the markedly higher valuations observed in Climate & Energy Series A rounds relative to seed-stage capital raised.

These two approaches triangulate to a range of $40M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $99M. The wide dispersion reflects tension between the stage-progression method—anchored to actual capital deployed—and the peer-median approach, which captures market pricing at the next stage. Key uncertainties include product maturity relative to peer companies, burn rate, and whether near-term financing will occur at seed multiples or command Series A pricing. A valuation in the $60M–$120M range reconciles these methodologies, though final pricing will depend on investor conviction regarding market timing and competitive positioning.
